pageTitle.value: Rolex Partnerships - Empowering communities with Asahi

pageCaption.value: The Asahi Shimbun launched SATO PROJECT for preserving the scenery of beautiful Japanese SATO (spiritual home of Japan) from environmental changes and depopulation.

metadescription.value: Rolex is partnering with Asahi to support innovative projects that improve inclusiveness and community protection. Discover more on rolex.org

text.value:
The Asahi Shimbun launched SATO PROJECT for preserving the scenery of beautiful Japanese SATO (spiritual home of Japan) from environmental changes and depopulation.

In partnership with Rolex as part of their Perpetual Planet initiative, this project will explore and disseminate, through information, reports and photographs, the issues that need to be solved in order to protect our SATO. In this issue, we introduce the projects of the Japanese Laureates of the Rolex Award who served as the selection committee of SATO PROJECT.

pageTitle.value: Rolex Partnerships - IT to enable inclusion

pageCaption.value: In 2016, at the age of 29, Junto Ohki received the Rolex Awards in the Young Laureates category (for those aged 18 to 30) for his project, "Expansion of the Cloud-based Online Sign Language Dictionary."

WaPageTitle.value: Building a society where the deaf play active roles

metadescription.value: With the help of IT technology, Junto Ohki's project empowers deaf people to have an active role in the society. Learn more on rolex.org.

previewTitle.value: Building a society where the deaf play active roles

pageTitle.value: Rolex Partnerships - Protecting communities from flooding

pageCaption.value: The Rolex Awards support pioneers who work on creative projects that make the world a better place.

WaPageTitle.value: Sowing seeds of rainwater harvesting globally

metadescription.value: Makoto Murase's initiative helps urban communities to prevent sewage flood damages by harvesting rainwater. Learn more on rolex.org.
